Primary Request and Intent: Three sections were built in this session for `page-opt-longevity.php`: **a) Buy Box section** — Nodes 5794:13082 (desktop) and 5794:13629 (mobile). Based on `#injections-section` from `mens-hrt-enhanced-pdp-1`. Left side = 2×3 image grid (desktop) / scroll-snap carousel + thumbs (mobile). Right side = "Optimal Health Biomarker Test" at $65, description, 3 left-bordered bullet points, yellow "Start Testing $65" CTA, HSA/FSA badge, 6 FAQ accordion items. **b) Disease Monitor section** — Nodes 5794:13878 (desktop), 5794:13667 (mobile), 6089:772 (floating card). Disease tag rows parallax like `womens-hormone-imbalance .monitor-for-early-indicators > .disease-list` (scroll-driven translateX). Floating biomarker data card overlays parallax on desktop as a modal; on mobile it becomes a standalone block sitting above the parallax rows. **c) Care Callout / Testimonials section** (IN PROGRESS AT SUMMARY TIME) — Nodes 5794:13236 (desktop), 5794:13783 (mobile). User said: "build the cards slider based on the reviews/testimonals section from the enhanced-pdp template we used earlier." Both Figma designs and the reference section from `mens-hrt-enhanced-pdp-1` were fetched, but the section has NOT yet been implemented. 2. Key Technical Concepts: - WordPress PHP page templates, `build/` output via `yarn build` - Slick slider (jQuery) with `accessibility: false` to avoid `[aria-hidden] { display:none }` reset conflict - Slick inline CSS pattern (inlined `.slick-list`, `.slick-track`, `.slick-slide` core rules per-section) - `initCarousel` Slick wrapper from `src/static/js/utilities/carousel.js` - `toggleFaqItem` accordion from `src/static/js/utilities/accordion.js` (auto-initializes on DOMContentLoaded) - Scroll-driven parallax via `requestAnimationFrame` + passive scroll listener, modulo-wrapped offset - Content doubled in PHP for seamless parallax loop (`array_merge($row, $row)`) - `desktop-only` / `tablet-and-smaller-only` global utility classes from `settings/layout.scss` - `$medium-screen-and-smaller` (≤1024px), `$small-screen-and-smaller` (≤600px) breakpoints - `colors.$color-yellow` / `colors.$color-yellow-dark`, `fonts.$STIX-Two-Text`, `fonts.$DM-Sans` - Figma asset URLs expire in 7 days — biomarker card built entirely in CSS + inline SVG instead - `extended-center-border-above` / `extended-center-border-below` for section connectors - `border-bottom` class for section separators - PHP local function pattern for reusable HTML (`function render_disease_monitor_card()`) 3. const thumbs = document.querySelectorAll('.buy-box-thumbs .thumb'); if (!carousel || !thumbs.length) return; const slides = carousel.querySelectorAll('.slide'); function setActiveThumb(index) { thumbs.forEach((thumb, i) => thumb.classList.toggle('active', i === index)); } thumbs.forEach((thumb, index) => { thumb.addEventListener('click', () => { const slide = slides[index]; if (!slide) return; carousel.scrollTo({ left: slide.offsetLeft - carousel.offsetLeft, behavior: 'smooth' }); setActiveThumb(index); }); }); carousel.addEventListener('scroll', () => { let closestIndex = 0, closestDistance = Infinity; slides.forEach((slide, i) => { const distance = Math.abs(slide.offsetLeft - carousel.offsetLeft - carousel.scrollLeft); if (distance < closestDistance) { closestDistance = distance; closestIndex = i; } }); setActiveThumb(closestIndex); }, { passive: true }); } ``` - Added `initDiseaseMonitorParallax()`: ```javascript function initDiseaseMonitorParallax() { const section = document.querySelector('.disease-monitor'); const rows = document.querySelectorAll('.disease-monitor .disease-row-list'); if (!section || !rows.length) return; const config = [{ speed: 0.15, dir: 1 }, { speed: 0.12, dir: -1 }]; let ticking = false; function update() { const rect = section.getBoundingClientRect(); const vh = window.innerHeight, sh = rect.height; if (rect.top > vh || rect.bottom < 0) { ticking = false; return; } const progress = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (vh - rect.top) / (vh + sh))); rows.forEach((row, i) => { const { speed, dir } = config[i] || { speed: 0.1, dir: 1 }; const half = row.scrollWidth / 2; const rawOffset = progress * half * speed; const offset = ((rawOffset % half) + half) % half; row.style.transform = dir === 1 ? `translateX(-${offset}px)` : `translateX(${offset - half * 0.5}px)`; }); ticking = false; } function requestTick() { if (!ticking) { requestAnimationFrame(update); ticking = true; } } window.addEventListener('scroll', requestTick, { passive: true }); window.addEventListener('resize', requestTick, { passive: true }); update(); } ``` - DOMContentLoaded now calls: `initGalleryScroller()`, `initMarquee()`, `initLongevityTimeline()`, `initProactiveLotties()`, `initRealMoments()`, `initHowItWorks()`, `initBuyBoxGallery()`, `initDiseaseMonitorParallax()` 4. The `.section-inner`'s `overflow: hidden` clips the wide parallax rows without clipping the card (card is small enough to stay within bounds). - **Parallax seamless loop**: PHP doubles each disease row's items with `array_merge($row, $row)`. # Figma Extraction Workflow How to efficiently extract design data from Figma for template creation. ## Overview The Figma MCP tools provide design context as React+Tailwind code. This must be **translated** — not copied — into the theme's PHP/SCSS stack. The Figma output is a reference for layout, spacing, colors, and typography — never use it as-is. ## Step 1: Get Screenshots First Always start with screenshots to understand the full page visually. ``` Tools to use: - mcp__figma-desktop__get_screenshot (for desktop app / no fileKey needed) - mcp__figma__get_screenshot (for API / requires fileKey + nodeId) ``` Get all three breakpoints: - **Desktop** frame (1440px wide) - **Tablet** frame (768px wide) - **Mobile** frame (393px wide) ## Step 2: Get Page Structure Use `get_design_context` on the top-level frame first. If the output is too large (common for full-page frames), it will return sparse metadata with node IDs. Then drill into individual sections. **Strategy for large pages:** 1. Call `get_design_context` on the top frame → get section IDs from metadata 2. Call `get_design_context` on each section individually 3. Focus on the Desktop version first — tablet and mobile are responsive adaptations ## Step 3: Extract Section-by-Section For each section in the Desktop frame, call `get_design_context` with: ``` clientLanguages: "php,html,css,javascript" clientFrameworks: "wordpress" artifactType: "COMPONENT_WITHIN_A_WEB_PAGE_OR_APP_SCREEN" ``` ### What to Extract from Each Section From the Figma output, capture: 1. **Layout structure** — flex direction, alignment, gaps, padding 2. **Typography** — font family, size, weight, line-height, letter-spacing, color 3. **Colors** — background colors, text colors, border colors 4. **Spacing** — padding, margins, gaps (in px) 5. **Asset URLs** — image constants at the top of each component 6. **Content text** — actual copy/headlines for the template ### What to Ignore from Figma Output - React/JSX syntax (translate to PHP) - Tailwind classes (translate to SCSS) - `data-node-id` attributes - `css-*` class names (Figma-generated, not real classes) - `fontVariationSettings` (use font-weight instead) - Overly complex image layering (multiple stacked images are usually one photo with Figma export artifacts) ## Step 4: Get Responsive Variants After completing Desktop extraction, get the **Tablet** and **Mobile** versions of key sections that change layout significantly: - **Hero**: Layout shifts from side-by-side to stacked - **Listicle**: Image/text stacking changes - **Cards/Stats**: Grid columns reduce - **Review carousels**: Slides per view changes Sections that scale linearly (FAQ, simple text blocks) usually only need padding/font-size adjustments. ## Step 5: Download Image Assets The Figma MCP provides image URLs as `http://localhost:3845/assets/...` constants. These are temporary. **For production:** 1. Download each unique image asset 2. Save to `src/static/media/images/[template-slug]/` 3. Use `.webp` format where possible 4. Name descriptively: `hero.webp`, `review-amedee.webp`, `step-1.webp`, etc. **For SVG icons/logos:** 1. Download SVG assets 2. Save to `src/static/media/images/` (shared) or `src/static/media/images/[template-slug]/` (template-specific) 3. Use inline SVG in PHP when the icon is simple and benefits from CSS styling ## Common Figma Patterns ### Multiple Stacked Images = One Photo Figma often exports a single photo as multiple layered `` tags (background fill, actual image, overlay). In production, this should be **one** `` tag with `object-fit: cover`. ### Font Variation Settings Figma outputs `fontVariationSettings: "'opsz' 14"` for DM Sans. Ignore this — the font files handle optical sizing automatically. ### Decorative SVG Patterns Some sections include decorative wave/dot patterns as masked SVGs. Evaluate whether these add enough value to implement. If yes, include as background images in CSS. ### Progress Bar Segments The quiz progress bar shows segments as individual `
` elements. The `quiz.js` utility generates these dynamically — don't hardcode them.

The Figma MCP provides image URLs as `http://localhost:3845/assets/...` constants. These are temporary.

**For production:**
1. Download each unique image asset
2. Save to `src/static/media/images/[template-slug]/`

## Step 5: Handle Image Assets

The Figma MCP provides image URLs as `https://www.figma.com/api/mcp/asset/...` constants. **These expire in 7 days — never use them directly in production PHP.**

**Pattern for assets that don't exist yet:**
- Reference the expected final path in PHP: `$theme_folder . '/static/media/images/' . $template_name . '/guide-health-hub.webp'`
- Add `style="background-color: #d2cec4"` inline on the parent container — if the image 404s, the bg color shows through and the placeholder looks intentional
- This keeps the PHP production-ready and lets designers drop the real files in without code changes

**Pattern for placeholders during development:**
- Use `https://placehold.co/{W}x{H}/{hex}/{hex}` (no #) with the correct bg color from the Figma design
- Only use placehold.co when you genuinely have NO correct asset and NO expected filename — prefer the bg-color fallback approach above

**For SVG icons:**
- Check `src/static/media/images/icons/` first — many common icons already exist
- Use inline SVG in PHP only when the icon needs CSS color overrides (e.g., changes on hover/active)

## Step 6: Build and Verify with Chrome DevTools MCP

Always run `yarn build` then use the Chrome DevTools MCP to verify the section renders correctly before moving on.

```
After build:
1. mcp__chrome-devtools__navigate_page (reload with ignoreCache: true)
2. mcp__chrome-devtools__evaluate_script — check section exists, images load, carousel initialized
3. Scroll to section: element.scrollIntoView({ block: 'center' })
4. mcp__chrome-devtools__take_screenshot
```

**Key things to check in evaluate_script:**
```javascript
// Image 404 check
imgs.map(img => ({ src: img.src, naturalWidth: img.naturalWidth, complete: img.complete }))
// naturalWidth: 0 + complete: true = 404

// Carousel init check
carousel.classList.contains('slick-initialized')

// Slide visibility check (aria-hidden bug)
slides.map(s => ({ ariaHidden: s.getAttribute('aria-hidden'), display: getComputedStyle(s).display }))
// display: "none" on aria-hidden="true" slides = global CSS conflict (see QUICK_REFERENCE)
```

# Figma Extraction Workflow How to efficiently extract design data from Figma for template creation. ## Overview The Figma MCP tools provide design context as React+Tailwind code. This must be **translated** — not copied — into the theme's PHP/SCSS stack. The Figma output is a reference for layout, spacing, colors, and typography — never use it as-is. ## Step 1: Get Screenshots First Always start with screenshots to understand the full page visually. ``` Tools to use: - mcp__figma-desktop__get_screenshot (for desktop app / no fileKey needed) - mcp__figma__get_screenshot (for API / requires fileKey + nodeId) ``` Get all three breakpoints: - **Desktop** frame (1440px wide) - **Tablet** frame (768px wide) - **Mobile** frame (393px wide) ## Step 2: Get Page Structure Use `get_design_context` on the top-level frame first. If the output is too large (common for full-page frames), it will return sparse metadata with node IDs. Then drill into individual sections. **Strategy for large pages:** 1. Call `get_design_context` on the top frame → get section IDs from metadata 2. Call `get_design_context` on each section individually 3. Focus on the Desktop version first — tablet and mobile are responsive adaptations ## Step 3: Extract Section-by-Section For each section in the Desktop frame, call `get_design_context` with: ``` clientLanguages: "php,html,css,javascript" clientFrameworks: "wordpress" artifactType: "COMPONENT_WITHIN_A_WEB_PAGE_OR_APP_SCREEN" ``` ### What to Extract from Each Section From the Figma output, capture: 1. **Layout structure** — flex direction, alignment, gaps, padding 2. **Typography** — font family, size, weight, line-height, letter-spacing, color 3. **Colors** — background colors, text colors, border colors 4. **Spacing** — padding, margins, gaps (in px) 5. **Asset URLs** — image constants at the top of each component 6. **Content text** — actual copy/headlines for the template ### What to Ignore from Figma Output - React/JSX syntax (translate to PHP) - Tailwind classes (translate to SCSS) - `data-node-id` attributes - `css-*` class names (Figma-generated, not real classes) - `fontVariationSettings` (use font-weight instead) - Overly complex image layering (multiple stacked images are usually one photo with Figma export artifacts) ## Step 4: Get Responsive Variants After completing Desktop extraction, get the **Tablet** and **Mobile** versions of key sections that change layout significantly: - **Hero**: Layout shifts from side-by-side to stacked - **Listicle**: Image/text stacking changes - **Cards/Stats**: Grid columns reduce - **Review carousels**: Slides per view changes Sections that scale linearly (FAQ, simple text blocks) usually only need padding/font-size adjustments. ## Step 5: Download Image Assets The Figma MCP provides image URLs as `http://localhost:3845/assets/...` constants. These are temporary. **For production:** 1. Download each unique image asset 2. Save to `src/static/media/images/[template-slug]/` 3. Use `.webp` format where possible 4. Name descriptively: `hero.webp`, `review-amedee.webp`, `step-1.webp`, etc. **For SVG icons/logos:** 1. Download SVG assets 2. Save to `src/static/media/images/` (shared) or `src/static/media/images/[template-slug]/` (template-specific) 3. Use inline SVG in PHP when the icon is simple and benefits from CSS styling ## Common Figma Patterns ### Multiple Stacked Images = One Photo Figma often exports a single photo as multiple layered `` tags (background fill, actual image, overlay). In production, this should be **one** `` tag with `object-fit: cover`. ### Font Variation Settings Figma outputs `fontVariationSettings: "'opsz' 14"` for DM Sans. Ignore this — the font files handle optical sizing automatically. ### Decorative SVG Patterns Some sections include decorative wave/dot patterns as masked SVGs. Evaluate whether these add enough value to implement. If yes, include as background images in CSS. ### Progress Bar Segments The quiz progress bar shows segments as individual `
` elements. The `quiz.js` utility generates these dynamically — don't hardcode them.

+### Critical: `accessibility: false` does NOT prevent aria-hidden

+`accessibility: false` only disables keyboard tab/arrow navigation. Slick **still sets `aria-hidden="true"`** on non-visible slides with `infinite: true`. Always add the `.slick-slide[aria-hidden="true"] { display: block !important }` override in the carousel's SCSS block.

---
name: slick-aria-hidden-fix
description: Global CSS hides Slick's aria-hidden slides; must override in SCSS for infinite carousels
type: feedback
---

Always add `.slick-slide[aria-hidden="true"] { display: block !important; }` inside any Slick carousel's SCSS block when using `infinite: true`.

**Why:** The theme's `main.min.css` has `[aria-hidden="true"] { display: none !important }`. Slick sets `aria-hidden="true"` on all non-visible slides when `infinite: true` (clones included). This makes the entire carousel content invisible. `accessibility: false` in Slick options does NOT prevent this — it only disables keyboard nav.

**How to apply:** Any Slick carousel with `infinite: true` or `centerMode: true` needs the override. Carousels with `infinite: false` are safe (no clones, no aria-hidden="true" slides).
